examples.title: Hash Table Insertion with Linear Probing
examples.description: Insert operation using linear probing for collision resolution
examples.code:
PROCEDURE Insert(T, k, v)
  i ← 0
  REPEAT
    pos ← (h(k) + i) mod m
    IF T[pos] is empty OR T[pos].key = k THEN
      T[pos] ← (k, v)
      RETURN pos
    ENDIF
    i ← i + 1
  UNTIL i = m
  ERROR "Hash table overflow"
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm inserts a key-value pair into a hash table using linear probing. If the calculated position is occupied, it checks the next position (i=1,2,3...) until an empty slot is found or table is full.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1/(1-α)) average case, O(n) worst case

examples.title: Hash Table with Separate Chaining in Python
examples.description: Implementation of hash table using separate chaining for collision handling
examples.explanation: This Python implementation uses a list of lists for separate chaining. The _hash function computes bucket index. Insert updates existing keys or adds new ones. Search and delete traverse the appropriate bucket's linked list.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) average, O(n) worst case when all keys collide
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n + m) where n = elements, m = buckets

examples.title: Hash Table with Double Hashing in C
examples.description: Implementation using double hashing for open addressing
examples.explanation: This C implementation uses double hashing with h1(k) = k mod m and h2(k) = 7 - (k mod 7). The probe sequence is h(k,i) = (h1 + i*h2) mod m, which helps avoid clustering compared to linear probing.

examples.title: Rehashing Procedure
examples.description: Algorithm for rehashing when load factor exceeds threshold
examples.code:
PROCEDURE Rehash(oldTable)
  newSize ← 2 × oldTable.size
  newTable ← CreateHashTable(newSize)
  
  FOR each entry in oldTable
    IF entry is occupied THEN
      Insert(newTable, entry.key, entry.value)
    ENDIF
  ENDFOR
  
  RETURN newTable
END PROCEDURE

PROCEDURE CheckAndRehash(T)
  IF T.count / T.size > 0.7 THEN
    T ← Rehash(T)
  ENDIF
  RETURN T
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Rehashing creates a new hash table with double the size and re-inserts all existing elements. This redistributes elements, reducing collisions and restoring O(1) average performance. The threshold of 0.7 is commonly used.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of existing elements
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for new table (in addition to old table during copy)
